test: SARS-CoV-2 rapid antigen tests (lateral flow / Ag-RDT)
reference_standard: Reverse-transcription PCR (RT-PCR), Ct cutoff specified per study
population: People undergoing testing for SARS-CoV-2 infection (community / outpatient / symptomatic / asymptomatic / mixed)

primary_tier.raw_quote: Of the 953 participants, 14.9% were positive for SARS-CoV-2 as determined by RT-PCR. The sensitivity and specificity were similar for both self- and clinician-performed RATs (sensitivity: 83.9% vs 88.2%, p=0.40; specificity: 99.8% vs 99.6%, p=0.6).

Of the 953 participants, 14.9% were positive for SARS-CoV-2 as determined by RT-PCR. The sensitivity and specificity were similar for both self- and clinician-performed RATs (sensitivity: 83.9% vs 88.2%, p=0.40; specificity: 99.8% vs 99.6%, p=0.6).

Of 663 participants included in the primary analysis, 39.2% (260/663, 95% CI 35.5% to 43.0%) had a positive RT-PCR result. The SD Biosensor POCT had sensitivity 84.0% (178/212, 78.3% to 88.6%) and specificity 98.5% (328/333, 96.5% to 99.5%).
the BD Veritor POCT had sensitivity 76.5% (127/166, 69.3% to 82.7%) and specificity 98.8% (249/252, 96.6% to 99.8%) compared with RT-PCR.

Testing was conducted at mobile community testing centres on 677 ambulant patients ... in the field study, of 146 RT-PCR positive individuals, 101 were RTD positive in the clinic. The RTD had a sensitivity of 69.2% (95%CI 61.4, 75.8) and specificity of 99.0% (95%CI 98.8, 99.3).

sensitivity_tier_added.country: East Africa (Tanzania, Uganda, Burundi, Rwanda, South Sudan)
sensitivity_tier_added.raw_quote: The two rapid antigen tests (Bionote NowCheck COVID-19 Ag and SD Biosensor STANDARD Q COVID-19 Ag) were evaluated against the detection of SARS-CoV-2 RNA by the Reverse Transcription PCR (RT-PCR) gold standard. Of the concordant results with both RT-PCR and rapid antigen test data (862 for Bionote and 852 for SD Biosensor), overall clinical sensitivity was 60% and 50% for the Bionote NowCheck and the SD Biosensor STANDARD Q, respectively. Stratification by viral load, including samples with RT-PCR cycle thresholds (Ct) of <25, improved sensitivity to 90% for both rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs). Overall specificity was good at 99% for both antigen tests.

_engine_expected_flags.note: These are documentation only - the engine recomputes at page-load
_engine_expected_flags.expected_fallback_combined: REML or FE bivariate (k=6 - REML may converge or fall back depending on small heterogeneous data)
_engine_expected_flags.expected_fallback_primary: REML or FE bivariate (k=5 - boundary case)
heterogeneity_axes: test_brand (BinaxNOW vs Standard Q vs BD Veritor vs Panbio vs Bionote)
geography (US/UK vs SA vs East Africa)
